reasoning: Nodirbek Abdusattorov was ranked #6 with a rating of 2773 as of the latest official FIDE rating list (April 2025). This gives him a 15-point cushion over the players below him (#7 Praggnanandhaa at 2758, #8 Wei Yi at 2758, #9 Nepomniachtchi at 2757, and #10 Firouzja at 2757) and he is closely trailing those above him (#5 Caruana at 2776, #4 Erigaisi at 2782). To drop out of the top 6, Abdusattorov would need to lose a substantial number of points, or one of the trailing players would need to have an outstanding tournament performance (+15 point swing), without Abdusattorov correspondingly moving up. Over the 1-month horizon for the May rating list (which dictates the May 4 and May 27 resolution dates), such large swings are uncommon at the elite level, especially with the 15-point gap bridging him from #7. Thus, the probability is very high for the near-term. For the longer term (July and October 2025), variance naturally increases. There is a cluster of four extremely strong players (Pragg, Wei Yi, Nepo, Firouzja) right around 2757-2758, making it reasonably likely that at least one of them will perform well enough to challenge the 2770+ sphere. However, as Abdusattorov is only 3 points behind Caruana, he is also positioned to move up. Adjusting for regression to the mean and standard rating volatility, the probability of him remaining top 6 slowly decreases to ~66% by October 2025.
